title: Convert legacy module syntax to Move 2024 file-level form
prompt: Update sources/legacy.move to use the Move 2024 file-level module syntax. Do not change function bodies; only change the module declaration form.

title: Migrate to @mysten/sui v2 JSON-RPC client
prompt: Update src/index.ts to use the @mysten/sui v2 SDK. The 1.x SuiClient is deprecated; switch to SuiJsonRpcClient from @mysten/sui/jsonRpc and pass the now-required network parameter (mainnet).

title: Implement the one-time-witness pattern for a currency module
prompt: Implement the one-time-witness (OTW) pattern in sources/demo.move so the coin::create_currency call in init() compiles. The OTW struct must match the module name uppercased (DEMO) and have only the drop ability. Pass the OTW by value into init.

title: Use Move 2024 method-call syntax for vector ops
prompt: Update sources/lib.move to use Move 2024 method-call syntax (`v.push_back(x)`, `v.pop_back()`) instead of the function-call form. Behaviour must be identical.

title: Replace a while loop with the vector::do! macro
prompt: Rewrite the summation loop in sources/lib.move using the Move 2024 `vector::do!` macro instead of a `while` loop.

title: Attach and read a dynamic object field on a Parent
prompt: Add two public functions to sources/lib.move: `add_child(parent: &mut Parent, key: u64, child: Child)` using `dynamic_object_field::add`, and `borrow_child(parent: &Parent, key: u64): &Child` using `dynamic_object_field::borrow`. Define a simple `Child` struct with `key + store` abilities.

title: Drop explicit Sui framework dep in favour of implicit (1.45+)
prompt: Update Move.toml to use Sui 1.45+ implicit framework dependencies — remove the explicit `Sui = { ... }` line entirely. Edition stays at 2024.

title: Apply the hot-potato pattern to a Receipt struct
prompt: Make the trade flow safe: ensure `Receipt` has NO abilities (hot-potato pattern), and add a `complete_trade(receipt: Receipt, ...)` function that destroys the receipt by-value via destructuring.

title: Create a TransferPolicy with a 5% royalty rule
prompt: Add a function `init_policy(publisher: &Publisher, ctx: &mut TxContext)` that creates a `TransferPolicy<MyNft>` via `transfer_policy::new`, attaches a 5% royalty rule, and shares the policy.

title: Write a multi-actor test_scenario test for a shared Pool
prompt: Write a `tests/pool_tests.move` with a `#[test]` function that uses `test_scenario` to: create the shared Pool, have actor ALICE deposit, then have actor BOB withdraw concurrently. Use `test_scenario::next_tx` between actors and `test_scenario::take_shared`.

title: Mint a Child via sui::derived_object
prompt: Add a `derive_child(parent: &mut Parent, index: u64, ctx: &mut TxContext)` function that uses `sui::derived_object` to create a `Child` with a deterministic UID derived from the parent's UID and the index.

title: Pick a random raffle winner with sui::random
prompt: Implement a `draw_winner(raffle: &mut Raffle, r: &Random, ctx: &mut TxContext)` function that uses `sui::random::Random` to pick a random index in the participants vector. Use `random::new_generator(r, ctx)` and `random_generator::generate_u64_in_range`.

title: Publish a blob to Walrus and anchor its id on Sui
prompt: In src/index.ts, implement `publishAndAnchor(blob: Uint8Array): Promise<{ blobId: string; suiObjectId: string }>` that writes the blob to Walrus via `WalrusClient.writeBlob`, then submits a Sui transaction creating a `BlobCommitment` object containing the returned blobId. Use the v2 SDK shape.

title: Encrypt a secret with a Seal capability-gated policy
prompt: In src/index.ts, implement `encryptForCapability(secret: Uint8Array, capabilityId: string): Promise<Uint8Array>` that uses `@mysten/seal`'s SealClient to encrypt the secret with a capability-gated decryption policy referencing the given capabilityId.

title: Refactor a u8-tagged state machine to a Move 2024 enum + match
prompt: Refactor sources/state.move to replace the `u8` tag with a Move 2024 `enum State` (variants `Idle`, `Running`, `Done`), and rewrite `step` to use exhaustive `match` over the variants.

title: Rename AdminCap to OperatorCap across three modules
prompt: Rename the capability struct `AdminCap` to `OperatorCap` everywhere it is referenced in this package: its definition in `sources/auth.move`, all uses in `sources/pool.move`, and the test in `tests/pool_tests.move`. Update every type annotation, every use-statement, and every constructor/destructor occurrence. The package must still compile after the rename.

title: Split a monolithic protocol module into state + entry
prompt: Split `sources/protocol.move` into two files in the same package: `sources/state.move` (struct definitions + view functions) and `sources/entry.move` (the public mutators `new`, `share`, `deposit`, `withdraw`). Preserve the public API and types unchanged. Update use-statements as needed. The package must compile.

title: Migrate a 3-file TS dApp from @mysten/sui 1.x to 2.0
prompt: Migrate this dApp from the `@mysten/sui` 1.x API to the 2.0 API across all three files. Specifically: replace the 1.x `SuiClient` from `@mysten/sui/client` with the 2.0 `SuiJsonRpcClient` (from `@mysten/sui/jsonRpc`) including the required `network` parameter; replace `TransactionBlock` from `@mysten/sui/transactions` with the 2.0 `Transaction` type; update every call site. Read `.ts-sdk-docs/sui/migrations/sui-2.0/index.mdx` first.

title: Attach a Treasury as a dynamic object field and expose a typed accessor
prompt: In `sources/pool.move`, implement the TODO: extend `new` (or add a separate entry function) that attaches a `Treasury` to a `Pool` as a dynamic object field keyed by `b"treasury"`. Add `treasury(&Pool): &Treasury` and `treasury_mut(&mut Pool): &mut Treasury` accessors. Then complete the corresponding test in `tests/pool_tests.move` so it asserts the Treasury balance via the accessor instead of aborting. The package must compile.

title: Improve the safety of a shared deposit/withdraw pool
prompt: Audit `sources/pool.move` and add safety improvements. The current implementation has no access control on withdraw, no per-address accounting, no events, and no bounds checking. Make the contract substantially safer while keeping the deposit/withdraw API usable. You may add new structs, capabilities, events, or invariants — your call. Justify any non-obvious design choices in comments.

criteria: correctness — implementation compiles and the new safety mechanism is semantically sound
idiomaticity — Move 2024 conventions: method-call form, capability shape, event past-tense names, no public entry
safety — withdraw is no longer unrestricted; accounting/caps/events meaningfully reduce attack surface
brevity — changes are scoped; no unnecessary new modules or complexity

title: Add a withdraw fee mechanism to the pool
prompt: Add a withdraw fee mechanism to `sources/pool.move`. The fee rate, storage shape (per-pool vs global config, basis points vs flat fee), and who receives the fee (protocol treasury, LPs, burned) are up to you — pick a coherent design and implement it end to end. The withdraw entry function must collect the fee atomically with the withdrawal.

criteria: correctness — fee is collected on every withdraw, accounting is consistent
idiomaticity — Move 2024 conventions, no public entry, balances vs coins used correctly
safety — fee can't be bypassed; no overflow on small/large amounts; no precision loss favoring the user
brevity — solution is proportional to the task; no over-engineered config plumbing

title: Apply Move Book naming conventions to a messy module
prompt: Clean up `sources/messy.move` to follow the Move Book code-quality naming conventions: error constants in EPascalCase (no ALL_CAPS error consts), capability structs suffixed with `Cap`, accessors named after their field (drop `get_` prefixes), mutable accessors suffixed `_mut`, and `setX` non-idiomatic setters replaced with mutable accessors where it makes sense. Keep the public API minimally broken — favor renames over deletions.

criteria: correctness — every renamed identifier is referenced consistently; the module still compiles
idiomaticity — error consts EPascalCase, caps suffixed Cap, no `get_` prefixes, mutable accessors named `_mut`
completeness — all five anti-patterns in the fixture are addressed, not just one or two
brevity — renames only; no scope creep into new features

title: Use sui::derived_object for a deterministic Entry under a Registry
prompt: Implement the TODO in `sources/lib.move`: add `public fun claim_entry(registry: &mut Registry, key: u64, payload: u64, ctx: &mut TxContext): Entry` that creates an `Entry` whose `UID` is deterministically derived from `(registry.id, key)` using the `sui::derived_object` module. Two callers passing the same `(registry, key)` must abort. Read `.sui-docs/develop/objects/derived-objects.mdx` before implementing — the API shape evolved past the model's training cutoff.

title: Migrate from JSON-RPC SuiClient to the @mysten/sui GraphQL client
prompt: Migrate `src/index.ts` from the JSON-RPC `SuiClient` (`@mysten/sui/client`) to the GraphQL-backed client documented in `.ts-sdk-docs/sui/clients/graphql.mdx`. Replace the import, the constructor, and adapt the two query functions (`balanceOf`, `ownedObjects`) to the GraphQL API. The functions must keep the same signatures.

title: Migrate a coin module from coin::create_currency to the Coin Registry
prompt: Migrate `sources/demo.move` from the legacy `coin::create_currency` flow to the newer Coin Registry pattern. Read `.sui-docs/snippets/coin-standards-migrate.mdx` (and surrounding docs) for the exact API. Remove the legacy `coin::create_currency` call and any subsequent metadata freeze; emit the registry-based equivalent. The package must compile against the current Sui framework.

title: Configure sui::display::Display for an NFT module
prompt: Implement the TODO in `sources/nft.move`: inside `init`, construct a `sui::display::Display<Hero>` using the claimed `Publisher`, add at least the `name`, `image_url`, and `description` keys mapped to the corresponding struct fields, call `update_version` on the Display, and transfer both the Display and the Publisher to the sender. Read `.sui-docs/develop/objects/display/using-display.mdx` for the current API. The package must compile.

title: Implement a feature from a long structured spec (token-pressure)
prompt:
Extend `sources/vault.move` to add the following feature set. Spec:

1. Capability: add a `VaultAdminCap` capability struct. Mint exactly one in a fresh `init(ctx)` function and transfer it to the sender. Only the cap holder may perform admin actions (pause toggle).

2. Per-address deposit ledger: instead of pooling all deposits, the vault must remember, per depositor address, the amount they have deposited so far. Use a dynamic field keyed on `address` storing a `Balance<SUI>` (or a tracked u64 — your call, document it). A second deposit from the same sender accumulates; a withdraw consumes some-or-all of that ledger entry.

3. Pause toggle: a boolean `paused: bool` field on `Vault`. `deposit` and `withdraw` abort with `EVaultPaused` if `paused == true`. Add `set_paused(&mut Vault, &VaultAdminCap, bool)` to flip the flag.

4. Events: emit `Deposited { who: address, amount: u64 }` after a successful deposit, and `Withdrew { who: address, amount: u64 }` after a successful withdraw. Event structs must be `has copy, drop`, named in past tense (per Move Book convention).

5. Edge cases:
   - withdraw of more than the depositor's ledger amount aborts with `EInsufficientLedger` (do not silently cap).
   - withdraw of zero aborts with `EZeroAmount`.
   - deposit of zero is a no-op (don't emit an event for it).
   - calling withdraw with an address that has no ledger entry aborts with `ENoEntry`.

6. Idioms: use Move 2024 conventions throughout (method-call form, no `public entry`, `EPascalCase` error constants, events in past tense). Use the `sui::dynamic_field` module for the ledger.

Acceptance: the package must compile (`sui move build`). Keep `deposit` and `withdraw` as the public entry points (signature may change). Do not introduce new modules.

title: /specify identifies the externally-reachable function set
prompt: Run /specify on the Move package in the current directory in non-interactive mode (SPECIFY_AUTO_DEFAULTS=1). Phase 0 may surface that sui-prover is not installed in this eval environment — in that case still run Phase 1 (discovery) and write .specify-progress.json with the externally-reachable function set, then exit. The goal of this task is to verify the discovery classifier, not the prover invocation.
